description:
I need to scrape some basic information from a webpage using a headless browser. Can you help me extract data from the Hacker News front page?

Please use a headless browser (like puppeteer with Node.js or playwright) to visit https://news.ycombinator.com and extract the titles of the top 5 stories currently displayed on the front page.

Save the results to a file at /home/user/hn_titles.txt with the following exact format:
- One title per line
- Each line should be prefixed with a number and a period, followed by a space, then the title
- Example format:
```
1. Some Story Title Here
2. Another Story Title
3. Third Story Title
4. Fourth Story Title
5. Fifth Story Title
```

The file should contain exactly 5 lines, numbered 1 through 5, representing the first 5 story titles in order as they appear on the page (from top to bottom).

After creating the file, also create a simple log file at /home/user/scrape_log.txt that contains:
- Line 1: The exact text "STATUS: SUCCESS" if the scrape completed successfully
- Line 2: The exact text "ITEMS_SCRAPED: 5"
- Line 3: The URL that was scraped in the format "URL: https://news.ycombinator.com"

Make sure both files are created with proper permissions so they can be read.
truth:
The automated test will verify:

1. File /home/user/hn_titles.txt exists and:
   - Contains exactly 5 lines
   - Each line matches the regex pattern: ^\d+\. .+$
   - Lines are numbered 1 through 5 in order
   - Each title (after the number prefix) is non-empty

2. File /home/user/scrape_log.txt exists and contains exactly:
   - Line 1: "STATUS: SUCCESS"
   - Line 2: "ITEMS_SCRAPED: 5"
   - Line 3: "URL: https://news.ycombinator.com"

3. Both files should be readable (at least 644 permissions)

Pre-installed tools that should be available:
- Node.js with npm
- puppeteer or playwright should be installable via npm
- Alternatively, python3 with playwright or selenium

The agent can use any headless browser tool available. Common approaches:
- npm install puppeteer && node script.js
- npm install playwright && node script.js
- pip install playwright && playwright install chromium && python script.py

The titles will be dynamic based on current Hacker News content, so the test should only verify:
- Correct number of lines (5)
- Correct format (numbered list)
- Non-empty titles
- Log file has exact required content

Directory /home/user should exist and be writable by the user.
